name: Easter Kulick with Paska
recipeInstructions:
Bread:
Dissolve yeast in warm water. Stir in milk, sugar, eggs, shortening, 2 cups flour, raisins, almonds, and vanilla. Beat until smooth with an electric mixer. Mix in enough remaining flour to make dough easy to handle. Turn out on a lightly floured board and knead, adding remaining flour until smooth and elastic (about 5 minutes). Cover with saran wrap and let rest for 20 minutes. Divide into halves and let rise about 1 hour until doubled. Place each half in a well greased 1-pound coffee can. Let rise to the top of the cans (about 40 minutes). Preheat oven to 375°F. Bake 40-45 minutes or until brown, drizzling with frosting while still warm and serve with Paska.
Frosting:
Powdered Sugar - 1/2 cup
Warm Water - 1 1/2 tsp
Lemon Peel, Grated - 1/2 tsp
Lemon Juice - 1/2 tsp
Paska:
Sweet Butter, Unsalted - 1/4 lb
Powdered Sugar - 1/2 lb
Egg Yolks - 4
Farmer Cheese - 3 lbs
Raisins - 1/2 lb
Nuts (Chopped) - 1/2 lb
Vanilla - 1 tsp
Cream - 1/2 pt
Cherries (Chopped) - 2 cups
Mix the butter and sugar together until smooth. Add remaining ingredients, mixing well. Put in a cheese cloth to drain liquid for about 3 hours using a weight. Remove from cloth and refrigerate.
